growth_records.strain: marine isolate (shallow-water hydrothermal vent isolate; 16S rRNA >99.7% identity to S. rubidaea)

up.method: Real-time dissolved-O2 fluorescence sensor spot in 24-well plate; O2 consumption as growth proxy
Real-time dissolved-O2 fluorescence sensor spot in 24-well plate

sec.method: Spectrophotometric quantification of extracted red product

yields.note: Product-from-biomass yield peaked 0.7 mg/g at 20 C; product titers 0.62 mg/L (MB) and 0.66 mg/L (F2S)

growth_records.cite: Pereira et al. Isolation and Characterization of a Serratia rubidaea from a Shallow Water Hydrothermal Vent. Marine Drugs (2023). DOI:10.3390/md21120599. [PMC10745058]
growth_records.method: reported qualitatively (grew fastest at 30 and 37 C by OD/biomass and dissolved-O2 depletion); no numeric mu given
growth_records.snippet: Both marine-based media, F2S and MB, allowed the highest product concentrations, which reached 0.66 and 0.62 mg/L, respectively... the highest product from biomass yield was observed at 20 C, reaching 0.7 mg/g... a faster decrease (correspo
growth_records.notes: S. rubidaea marine isolate, batch shaken-flask in Marine Broth (MB), 30 C (fastest growth + highest productivity; assays run at 30 C for pH study). MB composition not stated in text (commercial marine broth) -> composition:[] , medium_composition_stated:false; complex media were supplemented with 30 g/L NaCl (pigment only produced with NaCl). Measured O2 consumption reported only as % dissolved-O2 per hour (10.4% DO/h at 1:15 dilution, 7.4% DO/h at 1:40) - cannot convert to mmol/gDW/h. Prodigiosin secretion titers 0.62 mg/L (MB), 0.66 mg/L (F2S); product yield 0.7 mg/g biomass at 20 C. No numeric growth rate reported for 30 C.

sec.units: significantly decreased at 62 C (not quantified numerically)

yields.note: Biomass 0.85 g/L reached in first 1.8 h at 62 C; no further increase. Prodigiosin production significantly reduced at 37 and 62 C. Strain can produce prodigiosin up to 62 C

growth_records.cite: Pereira et al. Isolation and Characterization of a Serratia rubidaea from a Shallow Water Hydrothermal Vent. Marine Drugs (2023). DOI:10.3390/md21120599. [PMC10745058]
growth_records.method: reported (biomass 0.85 g/L attained within first 1.8 h then plateaued); numeric mu not derivable without initial biomass
growth_records.snippet: cells growing at 62 C attained a concentration of 0.85 g/L in the first 1.8 h, but no additional increase in cell number was observed... At 37 and 62 C, S. rubidaea cells significantly decreased the production of the red product... The S. r
growth_records.notes: Distinct thermal condition (62 C, hydrothermal-vent simulation) for the same S. rubidaea isolate, batch shaken-flask in MB. Biomass reached 0.85 g/L within 1.8 h then plateaued (Fig 4a). Growth rate not computable: initial biomass (t0) not stated, so mu=ln(X/X0)/t cannot be evaluated without fabricating X0. Prodigiosin titer at 62 C only described qualitatively as significantly decreased. MB composition not stated (commercial); complex media supplemented with 30 g/L NaCl. Included as separate record because temperature is a distinct experimental condition with a reported biomass endpoint.
